headline: A quiet bay *of crystalline water*, edged by palms.
lede: Mayungu Beach is one of the most spectacular stretches of the Kenyan coast — shallow, crystal-clear water; soft white sand; and at low tide, ribbons of sandbank that emerge offshore in vivid contrast to the turquoise sea. The private beach club of Exploreans Watamu sits directly on this bay.
paragraphs: The beach club is a 150-metre walk from the villas, through the property's tropical gardens. Guests have free use of the makuti-thatched gazebos and sunbeds, fresh-water showers, the beach restaurant, and the wellness pavilion.

The Watamu Marine National Park is a few minutes south. From the beach, a short paddle reaches the reef. Local guides arrange snorkelling, kayaking, dhow trips, and — at low tide — a walk out to the sandbanks that appear, briefly, in the open sea.

features.title: Makuti gazebos & sunbeds
features.description: Traditional thatched shade, complimentary for resident guests; rentable for non-residents.

features.title: Beach à la carte restaurant
features.description: A second on-property restaurant, directly on the sand under a thatched gazebo.

features.title: Beachside wellness
features.description: A second massage room at the beach club. Booking recommended.

features.title: Fresh-water facilities
features.description: Showers, changing rooms and toilets, kept simple and clean.

features.title: Direct reef access
features.description: Snorkelling, kayaking and small-boat trips into the Watamu Marine Park.

features.title: Low-tide sandbanks
features.description: White sand islands emerge offshore at low tide — walk out, swim back.

imageAlt: The private beach club at Exploreans Watamu — palms, makuti gazebos, and the open Indian Ocean.
backgroundImageAlt: Coastal refreshments and beach dining at Exploreans Watamu Beach Club
